name: AWS Cloud Infrastructure Compromise — IAM Privilege Escalation
description: A sophisticated cloud attack chain targeting AWS: initial access via exposed EC2 metadata service (SSRF), IAM credential theft from instance metadata, privilege escalation via misconfigured IAM policies, S3 bucket mass exfiltration, CloudTrail log deletion for anti-forensics, and persistence via new IAM admin user creation. Modeled on Capital One breach TTPs.

phases.name: SSRF to EC2 Instance Metadata Credential Theft
phases.description: Attacker exploits an SSRF vulnerability in a web application running on EC2 to query the instance metadata service (IMDSv1). The IAM role credentials (access key, secret, session token) are extracted and used for lateral movement within AWS.
phases.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API

phases.name: AWS Cloud Enumeration via Stolen IAM Credentials
phases.description: Using the stolen IAM credentials externally, the attacker enumerates S3 buckets, EC2 instances, Lambda functions, RDS databases, and IAM policies to map the attack surface.

phases.name: IAM Privilege Escalation via PassRole Abuse
phases.description: The attacker finds an IAM policy that allows iam:PassRole and lambda:CreateFunction. They create a Lambda function that assumes an admin role, bypassing permission boundaries and achieving admin-level access.
phases.technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Temporary Elevated Cloud Access

events: CloudTrail: CreateRole, AttachRolePolicy from external IP

phases.name: S3 Bucket Mass Exfiltration
phases.description: Using admin credentials, the attacker syncs all S3 buckets to an external storage. This includes customer PII, financial records, API keys in configuration files, and database backups totaling ~500GB.

events: CloudTrail: S3 GetObject spike from external IP

phases.name: CloudTrail Deletion — Anti-Forensics
phases.description: To cover tracks, the attacker deletes all CloudTrail logs, disables CloudWatch alarms, and removes GuardDuty findings. They also delete VPC Flow Logs to eliminate network evidence.

phases.name: IAM Backdoor User Creation for Persistence
phases.description: Attacker creates a new IAM user with AdministratorAccess policy and generates long-term access keys. A second persistence mechanism uses an EC2 user-data script to phone home on every instance launch.
